message:
test(storage): pin ft-ih4tm bidirectional FTS-trigger restore at the StorageHandle layer (ft-5n9my)

The SQL-level reversibility tests in fts_sync_tests
(`fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle`,
`fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip`) already pin the
`defer_fts_triggers: true` → `false` reopen-recreates-triggers
contract at the connection level. They run against a raw
`Connection::open_in_memory()` and exercise
`apply_recreate_fts_triggers` (the same `FTS_TRIGGER_RECREATE_SQL`
constant the production `else` branch in `StorageHandle::with_config`
uses), so they prove the trigger SQL is correct.

What they do *not* prove is that the production `with_config` path
actually reaches that branch on a real reopen of an on-disk database
through the full async/spawn-blocking writer-thread plumbing. A
reasonable refactor — moving the trigger-recreate into a closure that's
gated on `db_existed`, say, or accidentally short-circuiting it inside
a `cfg!` block — could break the integration without breaking the
SQL-level tests.

This adds `storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing`,
an end-to-end pin against the StorageHandle:

  1. Open with `defer_fts_triggers: false`, write a segment, shut down.
     Open the raw DB and assert all three triggers exist + the segment
     was synchronously indexed (1 FTS row for the segment's token).
  2. Reopen the same DB with `defer_fts_triggers: false`, write a
     second segment, shut down.
  3. Open the raw DB and assert: triggers still all three, the prior
     FTS row is undisturbed, the new segment was *also* synchronously
     indexed via the trigger.

The third assertion is the key contract — if a future regression has
the reopen path silently flip into deferred mode (the original ft-ih4tm
one-way-door bug), the second segment would not appear in FTS until a
catchup `sync_fts` runs, and this test would fail.

Also adds `#[derive(Debug, Clone)]` on `StorageConfig` so the test can
reuse the same config for both opens without rebuilding it field-by-
field. `StorageConfig` is a small POD-style configuration struct with
no resource handles, so Clone is a safe and natural extension.

Verification: passes against
`/tmp/ft-rv-p0-target/debug/deps/frankenterm_core-c1f30b6edd71ef35`
(`storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing ... ok`),
and the existing `fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle` and
`fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip` SQL-level pins still pass.

Family: ft-wk5fo (deferred FTS opt-in) → ft-ih4tm (reversibility fix)
→ ft-5n9my (full StorageHandle integration pin).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
